title: Site-Specific Execution Policy No Longer Permits Action
scenario: A clinical action is authorized at one site, but site-specific execution policy changes before the action is attempted at another site.
initial_state: The action was authorized under the original site's policy.
state_transition: The action is attempted under a site-specific policy that does not permit execution.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated under the current site-specific execution policy.
constitutional_evaluation: Execution must conform to the policy applicable to the site where execution occurs.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the current site-specific policy does not permit the action.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when site-specific policy does not admit execution.
clinical_action: Execute action under non-permitting site policy
